Elixir OTP. Cover · Contents Contents Cheatsheet

A BookBank Field Guide

Elixir OTP

Building systems that heal themselves — processes, GenServers, supervisors, and the quiet art of letting things crash.

In the voice of The Crafter — we build the ideas up one small, honest step at a time, the way you'd build an interpreter: intuition first, then the code, then the trade-offs.

What we'll build understanding of

Eight short chapters. Each one earns the next.

  1. 01Processes: The Actor Modelspawn · send · receive
  2. 02Let It Crashlinks · monitors
  3. 03GenServerthe generic server
  4. 04Supervisors & Treesrestart strategies
  5. 05DynamicSupervisorchildren on demand
  6. 06Registry & Namingfind a process by key
  7. 07Task & Agentthe lighter tools
  8. 08Applications & the Boot Treewhere it all starts
  9. ★Cheatsheetthe whole toolbox on one page
Before we start

OTP isn't a library you import so much as a way of structuring a running system. It's a set of battle-tested behaviours — GenServer, Supervisor, Application — that the Erlang/OTP platform has used to run telephone switches at nine-nines uptime since the 1990s. Elixir gives them a friendlier face. By the last page you'll read a supervision tree the way you read a function.
